mappings.rationaleAI: A core text on major energy types (A), devices for energy utilization (B), and electric power generation (C.7), including fossil fuels, nuclear, and bioenergy.
Nuclear fission (A.1), fission and fusion reactors (B.9), and nuclear-powered generators (C.7) are the central subjects.
Recurring energy sources -- solar, wind, water, biomass (A.2) -- and the devices that harness them, including windmills, turbines (B.2), and solar collectors (B.10), are examined.
Thermonuclear reaction, nuclear fission, and radioactivity are explored as primary energy sources (A.1).
Radioactivity as a primary energy source (A.1) and radioactive isotopes for nuclear energy utilization (B.9) are central topics.
Provides the physics underlying thermoelectric devices (C.1), heat exchangers and refrigeration (B.7), and energy conversion generally.
The engineering of energy utilization devices (B) -- including turbines, motors, and generators (C.7) -- is addressed.
Optoelectronic devices including semiconductor lasers and optical fibres (D.3) and lamps and lighting (C.5) are explored.
Magnets, electromagnets (B.5), and electric motors (B.5.b) that harness magnetic and electrical energy are examined.
Solid-state devices -- transistors, semiconductor diodes, integrated circuits -- for energy concentration and control are the focus (D.2).
The physics of wind and water turbines (B.2), hydraulic devices (B.4.d), and steam engines and turbines (B.4.a-b) is examined.
The chemistry of batteries and fuel cells (C.4) and chemical energy utilization devices (B.8) is addressed.
Solar energy as a recurring source (A.2) and devices for utilizing it (B.10) are central topics.
Chemical explosives (B.8.b) and the destructive applications of nuclear fission and fusion energy (A.1) are examined.
